prompt: In many countries today, parents are able to choose to send their children to single-sex schools or co-educational schools. Some people think that children going to single-sex schools have disadvantages later in life. To what extent do you agree?
essay:
Nowadays, parents have the options of sending their children to single-sex schools or mixed schools and it is thought that children attending single-sex schools will face some problems in the future.In my opinion, I completely agree with this point of view.It is obviously that children going to single-sex schools will not only lose out on communication skills with other gender but also the ability to consider something from different angles when they grow up.
 Firstly, students who learning in gender specific schools have fewer interactions with the opposite sexuality.This can be a challenge for them in their future because they will definitely have to work in a unisexual working environment where teamwork with people of the opposite gender is indispensable.Furthermore, it would have a negative impact on their performance as well as that of others since they are lack of experience of understanding and interacting with the other gender.Moreover, co-ed schools help to reduce the prejudice against the opposite sex because boys will find that a girl can be as brave as any boy and that gossiping is also common among boys.
 Secondly, co-educational school is beneficial for children as they expose them to different viewpoints.Since male and female have varied perspectives on issues and it results in the difference of handling scenarios.Co-education allows children to have opportunities to be involved in and gain an understanding of the opposite gender's opinions.For example, it was statistically proven in an educational journal in Nigeria in 2012 that most versatile youth in their respective places of employment are products of the mixed gender academy, they also tend to have stable emotions no matter the pressure from the opposite sex.
 In conclusion, even though parents these days tend to send their child to single-sex schools due to the future’s drawbacks of unisexual schools, I believe there is a lot to be gained by enrolling their children in a co-ed school such as improving mutual understanding with their opposite sex counterparts and the faculty of thought and reason.

prompt:
Some people believe that eventually all jobs will be done by artificially intelligent robots. 

What is your opinion?
essay:
As technology continues to advance, the majority of the jobs in future will be done by machines with artificial intelligence. Robots possibly can take most of the jobs; however, it is highly unlikely that occupations that require human contact can be substituted. This essay will discuss, why most of the jobs will be automated and only a few will be carried out by humans.

Automation is becoming increasingly prevalent and is likely to have a significant impact on the labour jobs market that does not require specific and complex human skills. Many companies will turn to artificial intelligence to automate processes and increase  efficiency as it is far less expensive than hiring, training and employing a human. For example, in many countries car wash stations have been automated that cost far less than employees. In addition, these stations work around the clock and require no vacations or holiday pay. For these reasons, occupations that can be automated will be replaced.

Conversely, there are some occupations that require a human touch and for this ,reason can not be replaced by artificial intelligence. These jobs include education, medicine, psychology and social work to name but a few. Robots will never be able to achieve the social and emotional capabilities that are required to perform these jobs.

To conclude, a significant number of human jobs will be conducted by robots in the future. While robots can take over the labour market jobs , occupations that require human skills will always exist. Ultimately, skilful jobs which are effectively done by people may not be done  by machines.

prompt: Consumers are faced with increasing numbers of advertisements from competing companies. To what extent do you think consumers are influenced by advertisements? What measures can be taken to protect them?
essay:
It is crucial to consider the impact that many advertisements from different business competitors have on their customers. This essay will explore some of the issues which have resulted from this effect and propose some measures that might address them.

Firstly, one concern about using advertising is that people are tricked into buying things that are not necessary for them. This is because they are enticed to catch up with the latest trends, especially from celebrities they have been following. As a result, people squander their hard-earned money, which should have been poured into their long-term financial investments, on extravagant commodities. To tackle this issue, governments should educate their people about the essence of living below their means. In doing so, people will ensure not only do they have a wealthy and perennial future, but they also contribute to reducing waste.

Secondly, another significant issue associated with advertisements is the risk of misled harmful products. The reason for this is that, nowadays, there are a lot of companies which merely chase their benefits recklessly. These businesses try to get their customers to buy their products as much as possible without considering the future repercussions that may occur, which is a real danger in the context of medical supplies. For instance, people could perish due to misusing drugs, but televisions and newspapers today are saturated with abundant strength-boosting pills. This problem could be mitigated by tightening the advertisement regulations to ensure many noxious ads are filtered before they reach end customers. 

In conclusion, advertisements from competing businesses have created two problems which are extravagance and detrimental products. However, these issues could be minimized if governments invest effort in improving citizens’ knowledge and stiffening advertising regulations.
evaluation:

The essay adequately addresses the given task by exploring the extent to which consumers are influenced by advertisements and proposing measures to protect them. It covers all aspects of the task and provides relevant arguments and evidence to support the ideas presented.

**Coherence and Cohesion (8)**

The essay is well-organized and structured. Transitions between sentences and paragraphs are clear and effective, maintaining a smooth flow of ideas. Connecting words and phrases are appropriately used to establish logical relationships between different parts of the text.

**Lexical Resource (7.5)**

The essay demonstrates a good range of vocabulary, but there are instances of inaccuracies and overuse of certain words. For example, "extravagant commodities" could be replaced with "unnecessary purchases." Additionally, the use of "perennial" in the context of financial investments is not entirely appropriate.

**Grammatical Range and Accuracy (8)**

The essay exhibits a variety of sentence structures, demonstrating grammatical range and accuracy. However, there are a few grammatical errors, such as the incorrect use of "which" in the second paragraph. Additionally, the use of passive voice in the conclusion could be reduced to enhance clarity and directness.

**Overall Band Score: 8**

The essay overall meets the requirements of the task and demonstrates a good level of coherence, lexical resource, and grammatical accuracy. While there are some areas for improvement in vocabulary and grammar, the essay provides a solid response to the given prompt.

**Feedback and Additional Comments:**

prompt: Every day, millions of tons of food are wasted all over the world. Why do you think this is happening? And how can we solve this problem?
essay:
There has been an ever-increasing trend that people discard an excessive amount of food waste from all corners of the world. Although this problem can be attributed to a great number of reasons, some feasible solutions can be considered to address this.

There are a number of reasons for this adverse tendency. The first reason for the rise in the amount of food waste is the increased supply of cheap and readily available food, which encourages people to buy more than they really need. For example, in order to increase sale revenue as well as compete with other brands, many fast-food restaurants nowadays have launched plenty of promotion programs such as “By 2 get 1 free”, which has attracted hundreds of customers and thus the huge amount of food waste will end up in landfills. Another reason is that in order to deal with busy lifestyles, people tend to buy food for the whole week rather than go to the supermarket on a daily basis. However, some kinds of fruits and vegetables easily become rotten within a few days and then thrown away.

A wide range of strong measures must be implemented, not only by the government but also by individuals, to combat food waste. This first solution is that the government can develop some action plans to raise consumers’ awareness of food appreciation. This could be done by getting people involved in global food campaigns such as Think.Eat.Save and anti-food waste campaign, helping their citizens to become more conscious consumers and when everyone is fully aware of this, they would think more carefully when it comes to shopping for food. In terms of each consumer, it is important to make a detailed shopping list before going to the supermarket. This way helps them to have time to prepare and decide wisely what products should be bought, which eventually saves a lot of unnecessary food.

In conclusion, Although food waste seems inevitable in today’s world, there are various ways to improve this problem.

prompt:
Some people think news has no connection to people’s lives, so then it is a waste of time to read the news in the newspaper and watch television news programs. 

To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
News from newspapers and TV is really important in our lives. A section of society thinks news does not matter and is a waste of time. While the news is super useful for staying informed, using it the wrong way can have some bad results. The upcoming article will delve into my ideology before making a conclusive stance.

To begin with, news is a vital tool for making people aware and teaching them important things. In other words, news outlets all around the world played a big role in sharing knowledge about the virus, like its symptoms, ways to stay safe, and how vaccines work. For instance, during the COVID-19 pandemic, news was the best way to learn about how the virus was spreading. This information directly influenced how the public acted, making them follow safety rules, get vaccinated, and help fight the virus together. Hence, the news is a crucial way to stay updated about uncertain events worldwide.

Nevertheless, there are some problems with news when it comes to spreading false information. Put differently, news channels might share material that is not true or is made up. They are often in a hurry to be the first ones to tell a story, and that can mean they do not check if the information is right. Also, these platforms can make it so that the public only sees and hears stories and ideas that agree with what they already believe. This can make society more split and stop people from having good conversations. Thus, the spread of false news can lead to confusion, mistrust, and harm to individuals and society.

In conclusion, having engaged in the subject matter, I agree that news is super important for keeping up with what is happening around us. However, we should remember that using it the wrong way can mess up the facts. Therefore, it is imperative to be careful when reading news online and check it from different places to avoid these problems.

prompt: The best way to teach children to cooperate is through team sports at school, to what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
People have different views about the best way to cultivate children's collaborative spirit. Although doing team sports is one of the efficient ways to achieve that, I believe teachers can find better solutions than this method.

On the one hand, team sports demand students to work closely and communicate frequently, which will improve children's cooperative skills. During this process, they will realize that everyone is born with strengths and shortcomings and only by working together can they have a competitive edge in the future. Also, doing team sports brings students more happiness than individual sports and help them to make new friends, which may motivate them to cooperate with others in the future.

On the other hand, other methods, such as working in groups to solve a practical problem, are also efficient to boost students' collaborative spirit. For example, there are a wide variety of interdisciplinary programs in many schools, which need the contribution of team members that are good at different subjects. Without joint effort, children will never be able to come to a satisfying solution. As a result, they will realize how important it is to cooperate with and learn from each other. Meanwhile, students working in these joint projects will also find it helpful to work together because it can enhance their problem-solving skills and boost their knowledge in all the subjects that are used. 

In conclusion, although taking team sports works well in teaching children the importance of cooperation, I think there are better ways that teachers can come up with to encourage their students to work with joint force.

prompt: Some people believe that studying at university or college is the best route to a successful career, while others believe that it is better to get a job straight after school. Discuss both views.
essay:
Whether a university’s role should be to prepare graduates for specific jobs or simply to educate without a particular vocation in mind is a debatable issue. This essay believes that universities fulfil both purposes. The essay will first discuss the function of vocational courses, followed by a discussion of more traditional tertiary education.

Some students have a very specific job in mind when they enrol at university. Medicine, dentistry and accountancy have clear career paths and they allow people to focus on and then undertake a certain profession upon graduation. This suits those who have decided on a career and it avoids them wasting time studying a more general subject like English. For instance, in the United States, you must complete four years of higher education before you can study law and many feel that these four years are a waste of time, that would have been better used focusing on their chosen discipline.

However, this is not suitable for everyone and many people have no idea what job they would like to do when they are 18. For these young people, it is better to do a non-vocational course, such as philosophy and simply add to their intellect without a career goal in mind. If they were forced to study a more practical subject, they are more likely to quit or become disillusioned because they don’t see the point of their chosen subject. For example, The Times recently reported that only 50% of law graduates actually want to become lawyers at the end of their studies.

In conclusion, the function of the college is to both prepare students for the workforce and provide knowledge and which one a person chooses should depend on their preference at the time.

Some people think that it is a waste of money for countries to host big sporting events like the world cup, and that the money would be better spent on other things. However, others think that hosting large sporting events has a clear, positive impact on a country.
Discuss both these views and give your opinion.
essay:
People have different opinions as to whether nations should spend the national budget on hosting international sporting events. Personally, I believe that the events have beneficial impacts on the host nations as a whole.
    Advocates of the former view might assert that spending on sporting events could hurt countries’ economies. Staging the international sporting events would incur enormous expenses, to the detriment of the host nations’ budgets. The expenditure on renovating their stadiums, implementing higher levels of security, and hiring performance artists is so significant that hardly can poverty-stricken nations afford it. For example, instead of spending billions of dollars on hosting the 2010 World Cup and then drowning in debt, South Africa should have been expended those budgets on addressing the physiological needs of their citizens, such as improving the standard of living and eliminating starvation.
    However, I support the view that nations might reap a multiplicity of benefits from staging those sporting events. Firstly, A wide range of employment opportunities are available during years of planning and investment, and this will contribute to a decrease in unemployment. Secondly,  enthusiasm and excitement are an indispensable part of these events; it is clear from this emotional boost that people of the host nations will experience a drastic improvement in their life satisfaction. Lastly, such events are conducive to the development of culture and tourism in the host nations. Social cohesion and cultural understanding are further developed, thanks to stadiums and arenas thronged with flows of people from all over the world.  
    In conclusion, I concur with the portion of people who believe that the benefits of hosting international events would outweigh the drawbacks, although it might dig immensely into countries’ economies.

In contrast, many people cannot afford those electric cars, so they use normal fuel  vehicles.

Excess use public transport leads to more traffic jam on roads for hours. This makes people feel uncomfortable as well as late to their works. So, it causes many disadvantages to normal middle class people who tends to use normal vehicles for their daily uses.

In conclusion, by the above aforementioned  points, the rise in fuel rates has various effects on normal people and it is not the best way to solve the environmental pollutions. However, the electric vehicles and growing more trees can solve the majority of the problem. The government should implement new awareness of pollution in schools can also results to decrease environmental problems

prompt:
Some people believe that robots will play an important role in future societies, while others argue that robots might have negative effects on society.

Discuss both views and give your opinion.
essay:
The last two decades have seen enormous changes in the way people's lives are affected by the expert systems, with many advances in this field. However, while these developments have brought many benefits to the world. Personally I believe that the benefits of the robots are likely to outweigh the negative aspects of it.

Some people believe that there are two main reasons why it could be argued that robots might have adverse effects on society in the future. The main issue is that people feel the robots will have a negative impact on the human workforce. The people will be replaced by machines and this will increase the unemployment rate, it leads to more serious problems such as poverty and increased crimes. Furthermore, physical and health issues are a major concern, so people become less active and this may result in some health problems like obesity.

However, others feel that I would agree with those who argue that robots will play important positive roles in future society. The first role is that robots could operate in locations that humans cannot reach such as exploration of the outer space.  Moreover, robots will be used inside the human bodies for monitoring their health and undertake surgery. The second role that will be played by robots is the helping of elderly and retired people with their daily life activities like cooking and cleaning. Finally, robots could do normal jobs quickly unlike human beings. Robots don not complain or get distracted or tired. They readily work twenty-four hours and require minimal maintenance.

In conclusion, there are convincing arguments both for in and against the role of robots in our future would, but I believe that the robots will add many benefits to the future society.

prompt:
Some people believe that the government should take care of old people and provide financial support after they retire. Others say individuals should save during their working years to fund their own retirement. 

What is your opinion?
essay:
People have different opinions when it comes to providing financial support and attention to elderly people after retirement. While some people believe governments are accountable for assisting these individuals, others argue that individuals should plan their retirement ahead of time. However, in my opinion, governments could provide better support to the senior citizens of their country.

In the past, most families supported their parents and grandparents during their old age, which is a fact that is changing in today's world. Now, more and more people try to avoid giving any support to their elders and this trend has been growing day by day. In this case, governments should make some effort to subsidize the cost of living for these old aged people, who could not earn money because of their higher age; furthermore, housing for the aged can be built at the cost of tax money, which could at least provide a comfortable shelter to these people. Consequently, senior citizens will not suffer due to poverty or homelessness.

Undoubtedly, it is a good idea that people should also plan their retirement ahead of time to avoid suffering at a later age. However, it would be extremely difficult for people who earn a lesser income every month to save some money for their future. Even though a large sum of money could be saved for this purpose, no amount of money could assure a sustainable life. Governments could play an important role here to implement some saving systems which could make life easier for elderly people. Consequently, this will benefit senior citizens to live a happy life.

- The vocabulary used in the essay is generally appropriate for the task.

**Grammatical Range and Accuracy: 6**

prompt: Some believe that people are naturally born as leaders while others feel that leadership skills can develop. Discuss both views and give your opinion

This fact has brought to our lives the debatable aspect whether leaders have an intrinsic power to carry it out or, on the contrary it is other skills which can be developed. I will discuss both sides and I will express my opinion in the following essay.
As far as leaders are concerned, it goes without saying that their attitude can promote the idea of having born with this characteristic in a very successful way. Additionally, many people do believe that there is an awful lot of children who demonstrate these traits from the kindergarten. Hence, albeit they have never learnt these manners, they will grow older accordingly, and this plays an extremely important role. Therefore, not only are there people who have shown the mentioned features from the childhood, but also there is a complex myriad of relationship between management and personality. They are strongly interwoven, and would be really difficult to distinguish them.
On the other hand, it seems to be clear that had it not been for experience and exposure, many people would not have achieved to develop certain traits. In this case, it is highly remarkable that a staggering amount of people have boosted their authority abilities satisfactorily through training. For instance, by reading or even practising, we are quite likely to learn how doing it. It is, thus, when it comes to leadership, strongly indispensable to take into account the strong power of the discipline and diligence.
In the light of above exposed, I am inclined to believe that despite the fact many people could have borne with these characteristics, it is perfectly understandable that we could learn how we do it if we persevere in it.

prompt: Human activities have a negative effect on plant and animal species. Some people say that it is too late to do anything. Some people think that we should take effective action to improve the situation. Discuss both sides and give your opinion. ??

There are controversial perspectives heating up a debate over how to solve the negative effect issue caused by human activities on plant and animal species. While some hold a strong view that it is too late to measure the problem, the opposite makes a statement that we should enhance the situation with effective actions. I would contend that take effective actions to revamp the matters, which do more good than harm.

 It is obvious to note that there are many reasons to convince that it is too late in order to solve the negative effect of human activities on plant and animal species, one of them is deforestation. Having an ever-growing demand for land to live and wood to consume, humans decide on deforestation to serve their purpose. Therefore, it leads to flora and fauna being indiscriminately destroyed and lost completely instead of being replaced and it pushes many categories of species on the verge of extinction.  For example, Panda is China’s national treasure, but this species is on the brink of extinction because of deforestation. There is a wealth of animals in the world that are dying out because of human activities, which is the reason people doubt that it is too late to tackle the problem. 

 While the redeeming features of negative effects of human activities on flora and fauna species are widely acknowledged, we will find an address in order to enhance the issue. Plant and animal species play a paramount significant role in human’s life, so we should find a solution as soon as possible. One of the remedies is to build a reserve to create a safe habitat for endangered animals. For instance, Cuc Phuong national park in Vietnam is built and overseen by the authorities, which is home for a wave of precious animals. Hence, the situation will be resolved and revamped if people actively implement it.             

 In conclusion, although there are many reasons to prove that it is too late to deal with the negative effect on plant and animal species caused by human activities, people should take effective action to improve the problem.

In recent years, the question of whether art should be taught at school has been a matter of debate. Some maintain that art should be an integral part of the study; however, there is another school of thought which dismisses this notion as they deem that it is the dissipation of time. In this essay, I will discuss both assertions and conclude that it is of paramount importance.

The most powerful argument in favour of art being on the school curriculum is its profoundly positive impact on the development of children’s cognitive skills. By participating in art activities, children deploy a wide range of accomplishments, like decision-making, thereby enhancing them. Not only can art help children have a fertile imagination, but also promotes lateral thinking in them. It can, therefore, have far-reaching implications, as this expertise are also crucial to learning other subjects; hence it appears logical to suggest that art improve outcomes in all subjects.

Notwithstanding  these substantial benefits, it seems this subject is of no consequence to some. They point out schools should prioritise core subjects, like physics, arguing STEM skills are vital for students’ future lives and art is worthless. Given the fact that not all pupils have the opportunity to pursue their interest in art academies or at home, was art excluded from the school curriculum, they would not be able to understand the value of the arts, nor would those who are artistically inclined hone their skills. 

In conclusion, I subscribe to the theory that art should be a part of the curriculum in view of the fact art education is critical to students’ personal and educational development, and it is inextricably linked to other disciplines as it can foster those skills also needed for them.

It’s an undeniable fact that today’s youth is the future of the nation. However, I agree to a large extent with this opinion that for growth and development, it is important to invest more resources in its youth is the best way for a country to prepare for the future. This piece of writing will explore various dimensions and elucidate distinctive assumptions regarding the statement in the looming paragraphs.

First and foremost, investing in the education of young people should be the primary focus. Educated people will be an asset for economic growth. Secondly, schools should focus on educating children making them career-driven and preparing them to learn financial management. Furthermore, this will foster innovation, responsibilities and creativity which is quintessential for the country's growth. Additionally, not only education, health and well-being should also be the primary focus area. Health individuals will be more productive and contribute more to the economy According to a report by the World Health Organization, good health can boost economic growth by up to 4% a year.

Despite the fact, focusing solely on young people may not be enough. For instance, investments in infrastructure can facilitate trade and improve access to services, boosting economic growth. Moreover, investments in technology can increase productivity and create new industries. Lastly, investments in healthcare for all age groups is essential for a healthy and productive population.

To conclude, different people have different notions regarding the statement, but I have a plethora of reasons to advocate that although investing in the younger generation has huge benefits however, investing in other resources should not be overlooked.

To prove this, there are a lot of IVF and test tube baby centres available for such married couples. Therefore, it is advised by the senior citizens in  society to have a youngster soon after the wedding to avoid such fitness problems later.

In conclusion, babymaking at above 32 years of duration has both merits and demerits. However, giving birth to the baby at youth surely surpasses the possibility of abnormal well-being in reproduction.

prompt: As well as making money, businesses also have social responsibilities. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
It is true that businesses need to make a profit in order to not declare bankruptcy. Some people have opinions about whether businesses should have public responsibilities. From my perspective, I totally agree with the statement.

To begin with, it seems logical that businesses must make a profit in order to cover their overhead cost. Without profiting from their business, companies might not be able to cover their running costs, such as employees’ salaries, facilities, and payments for the building. As a result, companies might declare bankruptcy, and end up closing down their businesses. Another argument is that without making profits, businesses might not be able to fulfil their social responsibilities. This is because when businesses can not cover their running cost or fulfil customers’ demands, any social concerns might be neglected. In other words, less attention might be paid to public concern, simply because this is not businesses’ main purpose. This means if a business is not in good financial health, it might not be able to fulfil public concerns. 

On the other hand, businesses should also fulfil their public responsibilities, instead of focusing on the sole purpose of maximising their profits. Firstly, one social concern that is associated with managers and businesses is to treat their employees well. Treating employees right might provide them with the comfortability needed to improve their work efficiency, boosting the country’s economy. Lastly, I believe that companies should stop abusing accounting loopholes to minimise their taxes. Paying taxes is an essential part of businesses’ social responsibility. Instead of using accounting loopholes to avoid taxes, companies should happily pay taxes to contribute to society. 

In conclusion, it is logical that companies must make a profit to not go bankrupt, however, they should still fulfil public responsibilities instead of focusing on maximising profit.

Others, however, think that individuals themselves should save money in their own when they are old. Discuss both views and give your opinion. > DISCUSS AND OPINION
essay:
Some people believe that the government should take care of old people and provide financial support after they retire. Others say individuals should save during their working years to fund their own retirement. What is your opinion? Give reasons for your answer and include examples from your own experience.

It is true that when people advance into their golden years, they require more help and attention. For that reason, a significant number of people hold the opinion that the government should provide financial assistance to the elderly. However, I tend to agree with those who argue that workers should be saving a portion of their income to have a nest egg in their retirement.

To begin with, a culture of saving rather than depending on the government may foster the overall economic development of a country. In other words, if people were to accumulate their own savings for their pension age, it would lessen the financial burden on the government. As a result, the government would be able to release more funds to more productive purposes, such as development of infrastructure, industries, hospitals, and so on, which may expedite the overall progression of a country.

In addition to economic factors, putting money aside has some personal benefits. Since savings-oriented people are not dependent on the state for their living expenses, this may instill a sense of self-respect and pride in them. On the other hand, those who are reliant on financial assistance very often face the negative societal stigma, as many people consider them to be inferior and unworthy members of society. Hence, to live a life with dignity and pride, taking responsibility for funding one’s own retirement is evidently a better option from individual perspective.

In conclusion, to decrease the accidents and make sure the security of motor traffic, cyclists and pedestrians we should have excellent roads as well as a formal committee that responsible for the guidelines. Museums can be used to attract tourists which will benefit the country in the end as it can flourish the economy. To begin with, museums attract numerous tourists from all around the globe. From an economic perspective, museums generate job opportunities in the market and contribute to the growth of the local economy because of tourists, which will benefit the nation in the end. A museum where people could enjoy and entertain attracts more people than other museums. There are museums where it is not only about history or art, such as the kimchi museum in South Korea and the chocolate museum in Belgium. Hence, since these museums are quite popular among international tourists, museums should be a place to entertain people. 

On the contrary, museums play a crucial role in educating the public about art, history, science, culture among others. Moreover, it is imperative for educating future generations about their history, culture and roots. Thus, museums are undoubtedly a huge source of knowledge, so it ought to be solely used for educational purposes.

In conclusion, museums and entertainment purpose both for educational and entertainment purposes. From an economic point of view, enjoyable museums attract a significant number of tourists which will eventually benefit the country, while it is also a huge source of knowledge and plays an important role in educating people about art, history and culture.

prompt:
Some believe technology has made our lives too complex and the solution is to lead a simpler life without technology.

To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
Nowadays, many are of the opinion that technology has complicated our lives and that living without it would make life so much simpler. Although I agree that technology has drawbacks, turning our backs on it would be a huge setback for mankind.

To begin with, technology has played a crucial role in changing human communication, lifestyle and work for the better. Firstly, thanks to the invention of the Internet, people can now be updated with current affairs faster than ever. In addition, social apps such as Facebook and Twitter have allowed them to be in touch with their close ones more often with just a press of a button. Secondly, advances in broadcasting have provided numerous forms of entertainment. This greatly benefits individuals as well as improves their quality of life. Finally, the rise of automation has significantly reduced labour work and increased productivity. For example, manual tasks such as calculating or sorting, and memorizing can now be done by cutting-edge computers with high precision and reliability in a relatively short time. 

Even though technology has made our lives become more complicated and it is a negative trend, there are several measures to help to alleviate the problem. One of the most effective ways is to change individuals’ attitudes toward technology. Indeed, instead of considering it as an indispensable part of our life, people should treat technology as a tool with the sole purpose being to ease their lives. By doing so, they will not become too dependent on it and free their minds. Another possible way is returning to low-tech devices. To be specific, studies have shown that people may be happier and more satisfied if they are given fewer options in life. Hence, by using devices with few quirks and features, it is likely that they may experience a more fulfilling life, as well as appreciate what they have. For example, instead of using smartphones, millions of teenagers in Europe are now buying dial phones, also known as dumb-phones, because these help them to stay away from the dramas on social media, control their lives better, and see the world with more grace

In conclusion, while I agree that technology is making our lives more complex, humans will not likely lead a simpler life if we reject it. Instead, we should change our mindsets toward it and try to use low-tech devices rather than chasing after the latest technologies to live healthier and simpler.

For instances in Asian countries people believe that men are bread winners of the family as well as invest in their education but on the other hand women are taught to take care of the home and perform domestic work. Many women activist across the globe have brought up this issue to the society and fought gender equality in job market. As result of this, today many corporate companies incorporated polices to ensure their work force have equal number of women and men employee. Other than this IT sector need diligently transform their corporate culture to respect all employees, moreover make discrimination and gender inequality socially unacceptable. These changes will make the womenfolk feel more secure and boost their morality, thus we can perhaps close the gap between gender disparity.

Despite of these poor facilities they live happily with their family without complaining about them. For example, the children without a lot of money travel mostly by bicycle and public transports from their childhood, so they would not find it difficult to travel in their adult life compared to the wealthy children.

The next important reason for me to agree with this opinion is that they are better with time management and money management skills. These children help their parents to overcome the financial burden by doing  local errands for money which help them to manage time in adult life.For instance, perhaps during their childhood days they help their parents with the household work , after they are finished with school.Furthermore, middle class children tend to work part time in their neighbourhoods to help their family financially.

In conclusion , children with a middle class background have better management skills which are crucial for facing adult life.Unlike the children with wealthy parents , they have a better emotional stability to deal with the problems of adulthood.

prompt: Some people believe that the government should not spend money on international aid when they have their own disadvantaged people like homeless and unemployed. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
Over the last 100 years, population has grown at an exponential rate across many countries in the world. This brings along with it its own problems such as poverty, illiteracy, unemployment and so on. Many governments allocate huge resources to tackle these problems. Since all governments have problems to solve in their own countries, I agree with the argument that their first priority should be their own people.

There are ample reasons why I believe that the government should put the welfare of its own people ahead of others. To begin with, financial resources are becoming scarce and many countries are already in debt. These funds must be reserved for the development and maintenance of its own institutions and social security measures such as free health care and shelters for homeless people. Secondly, such welfare schemes not only elevate people out of poverty; they also help the government indirectly by creating jobs. This, in turn, facilitates the economic growth of the country.

On the other hand, it is understandable and reasonable to donate money to the poorer nations as a gesture of basic human decency and humanity. No nation, after all, can live in prosperity when its neighbours are struggling with poverty. By offering financial assistance to their poor neighbours, countries can also put a curb on illegal immigration. Even so, in my opinion, solving problems in one’s own country is more important than venturing to help others. Others should be helped only when the donating country has a surplus of money and resources.

In conclusion, there are obvious benefits for the government in the long run from donating money as relief aids to many foreign countries. However, I strongly iterate that the government must first improve the economic, financial and employment prospects of its own people before embarking on humanitarian work abroad.

prompt: Whether or not someone achieve their aims is mostly by a question of luck. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
Many people claim that luck is the most crucial element contributing to a person's success. While I accept that luck does have an important role in helping people reach their targets, I would argue that hard work and determination are much more crucial factors leading to the achievement of an individual. 

On the one hand, I agree that luck can be an important factor. First, some people are fortunate enough to be born in a wealthy family, which allows them to have a better education or to easily start their own business without having to borrow money from someone else. As a result, these people tend to have better opportunities to succeed than those coming from poor families. Furthermore, some individuals are lucky to be given great talents which others do not have. For example, Mozart was able to play music when he was very young, or Lionel Messi already had great football skills when he was born. 

However, I would argue that although luck can be necessary, it is not the most crucial factor leading to success. Firstly, people need to work really hard and put much effort into their jobs if they want to be successful. For instance, before becoming one of the best football players in the world, Cristiano Ronaldo had to go through intense training hours every day for many years. Secondly, a person is less likely to reach his targets if he does not have determination. On the way leading to success, there might be many difficulties, and people need to keep moving forward to overcome those challenges. 

In conclusion, while I agree that luck is important to some extent in helping people become successful, I believe that hard work and determination are the most important elements.

Moreover, cartoon and animated series target young children, and eventually affects their health as the time spent outdoors is considerably less.

On the other hand, it is believed by some that watching useful programmes has beneficial effects on children. One reason why it is helpful is that it gives them quality leisure time from the highly demanding various subjects. This gives them the chance to take their mind out and enjoy some fun time, and also cultivate their interests by watching suitable series. Another reason is that they could relax at the comfort of their home by watching their favourite shows or scientific programs, rather than venturing out, thereby managing time efficiently.

In conclusion, while people may vary in their opinions, I completely believe that spending time before the big screen has harmful effects on children's life.

essay:
It is commonly known that many species have gone extinct throughout history, including the dinosaurs. Some argue that preventing extinction is not necessary, as it is a natural process. However, I believe that humans have a moral obligation to protect endangered species and prevent their extinction.

Firstly, human activities such as deforestation, overfishing, and pollution have caused a rapid decline in animal populations, leading many species to be at risk of extinction. As a result, humans have a responsibility to conserve the environment and prevent further harm to wildlife. It is unfair for humans to cause the extinction of a species due to their actions, particularly when they have the ability to prevent it.

Another reason is that many species play a crucial role in maintaining the balance of the ecosystem. For example, bees are essential pollinators that are responsible for pollinating 80% of flowering plants, and thus if bees were to become extinct, it would have a devastating impact on our food supply and ecosystem. Similarly, the loss of predators can cause a ripple effect, leading to overpopulation of other species and causing imbalances in the food chain.

Lastly, preventing extinction is not only a matter of responsibility but also a matter of morality. Species have intrinsic value, and it is not our place to determine which species should exist and which should not. Humans must respect the inherent value of all life forms and do what they can to protect them.

In conclusion, while extinction may be a natural process, it is not a justification for humans to sit idly by and watch as countless species go extinct. By taking action to conserve the environment, humans can ensure that future generations can enjoy the same diversity of life that we have today.

prompt: As society changes, more and more people do not want to stay in one career or job. Many people change their careers and jobs. What is the cause of this phenomenon? Can you give some suggestions to solve this problem?
essay:
With the development of our society, there is a noteworthy phenomenon that more and more people do not stay in one job position in all their careers, which means people now change their job frequently. This essay will give several factors that why this happened and will give some suggestions to solve this problem.

The first reason why people changed their job frequently is due to the job market nowadays is much better than it was in the past. Now, there are more jobs for people to choose from in the job market than in the old times. So people will have more chances to join  a new position in the same field or switch to a completely new field due to  society's development.

The second reason is that the cost for people to live  a decent life costs much more than it  used to. So people always want their salary incremented each year. The best way to achieve this is to switch their job to a different company. That's the second reason why employee changed their jobs 

frequently.

The third factor is that the employee are easier to get bored with what they have done in the company, and thanks to the development of the internet. People have a way to find what they are interested in and the internet helps people  learn new knowledge or technology easily. It provides a convenient way for people to switch their jobs. It also causes people  not to stay in one position for a long time.

But this phenomenon may do a little harm to the company because it may lose some expertise employees and if they want to hire  new employees with the same quality, it requires them to pay more salaries to them, which may influence the company's development and profits. So there should have some measures to alleviate this phenomenon happened. The company could provide a clear path for employees to get promoted and give some employees who perform well a good bonus. It will  make employees feel safe and satisfied. What's more, a good work-life balance is also a key factor to reduce the leaving rate for employees.

prompt: Some people think that one of the best ways to solve environmental problems is to  increase the cost of fuels for cars and other vehicles. To what extent do you agree or disagree  with this statement?
essay: Over the past decades, environmental problems have never failed to draw public attention  and provoke debates. Although the severity and urgency of addressing environmental  problems properly are hardly arguable, the issue as to whether governments should deal with  these issues with higher price of fuels or not remains controversial. From my own perspective,  I consider this approach is ineffective.  Firstly, it is important to recognize that although the occurrence of positive outcome towards environment is almost inevitable, the final result may not be as expected for long. Some may  hold optimistic attitude that this particular approach would be the effect on limiting the  number of cars and thereby reducing the amount of environment-damaging gas emission. This  argument could be true to some extent. However, they should take into account the fact that  since the cost of public transport would also be affected, resistance in people with the need of  travel seems to be inevitable, hence the impossibility of achieving the desired goal. Therefore,  increasing fuel prices will not make any significant changes.  Secondly, it is equally important to recognize that there is evidence that waste gas from cars is  not the leading cause of air pollution. The opponents may argue that the combustion of fossil  fuels by cars is the main producer of carbon dioxide. Nevertheless, they are ignorant of the fact  that the chef culprit is the discharge of pollutants into the atmosphere due to the rapidly growing  manufacturing industry. Governments could better handle this problem if they could increase  control over industrial waste-discharge and adopt more environmental friendly materials and  production equipment. For example, wind and tidal power in the Netherlands, nuclear power in  Japan and solar power in the United States have all proven their efficiency  in energy  production. This example indicates that other solutions could be employed in other parts of the  world to minimize the global dependence on fossil fuel.  In conclusion, under no circumstances should increasing the price of fuels be taken as the best  solution to environmental issues, as it has neglected both the reaction of consumers and the  root cause of the problem as mentioned above.

Nowadays, more and more people decide to have 

children later in their life. What are the reasons? Do the 

advantages of this outweigh disadvantages?
essay:
In recent years, a noticeable shift in family planning has emerged and many people prefer to have children at their later stage in life. Several reasons are responsible for this, and I believe that it has more consequences. 

Firstly, couples want to spend quality time together before having their first child. This is because they can enjoy their life with each other, and be able to know one another. In this way, mutual understanding becomes stronger, and enjoy the time they spend with their young ones. For example, research studies have shown that couples who spend more time together understanding each other can later take good care of their babies and there is also less chance of divorce rate after having children birth.

Secondly, people choose to concentrate on their careers first and then have babies it is because they can take better care of their juveniles. Additionally, they think having children can escalate the expenditures and financial responsibilities of parents. Therefore, they achieve a stable financial position before starting the family as a result this can enable parents to give better education and lifestyle to their children. For instance, parents would be capable of enrolling their juveniles in schools where they get quality education and learn co-circular activities, which are not available in all schools.

However, the drawbacks can not be underestimated. The main problem caused by giving birth later is related to the fertility of both genders. After a certain age, sometimes their fertility rate starts decreasing, due to which they face difficulties in conceiving babies, and pregnant women require special care. To exemplify, studies show that children who are born in later years are often born with some abnormalities, either in terms of physical or mental. Thus, they face problems to survive in this world.

In conclusion, although there are various merits of giving birth to a baby, a single disadvantage is more dangerous for both mother and child as they are sometimes prone to death.

prompt:
Men and women are different in terms of their characteristics and abilities. For this reason, some jobs are better done by men and others by women.
Do you agree or disagree?
essay:
These days, it seems that more women are entering “male occupations” than ever before and vice versa. While some still hold to the conservative view that certain jobs are better performed by men and others by women, it is my personal belief that people of their sex are capable of doing any job effectively. The reasons for my position are as follows.

First of all, I believe it is a mistake to base our views on broad generalisations about the characteristics of men and women as such stereotypes are often inaccurate. Although there are certainly traits which are typical of men and others which are typical of women, there are exceptions to every rule. Not all women are physically weak, just as not all men are poor interpersonal communicators; conventional gender roles are not appropriate for everybody. If a man and woman desires to pursue a career which is traditionally reserved for the opposite sex, I believe he/ she should be provided the opportunity to prove him/ herself capable.

Secondly, I would contend that the presence of both males and females in a workplace brings a sense of balance. Men and women frequently, though by no means always, have different methods of doing things. Rather than judging these differences to be weakness, I feel that we should view them as opportunities for innovation and the discovery of more effective and efficient work practices. A person’s weakness can thus be transformed into strengths.

In summary, I once again reaffirm my position that both male and female employees are an asset to any industry and as our stereotypes about men and women are not always appropriate, we must not discourage anyone from choosing an occupation simply because of his/ her gender.

prompt: Detailed description of crimes on newspaper and TV can have bad consequences on society, so this kind of information should be restricted on media. To what extent do you agree or disagree with this statement?
essay:
Many people have expressed that a number of reports detailing crime news should be controlled, as they have many negative effects on society. I partly agree with this view.

On the one hand, I believe the bad influences that stem from two main reasons. Firstly, the direct result of crime news with extremely details are to bring victims psychological injury. To pique readers’ interest, the media may use the bloody images and exaggerated language. This reminds me of a school murder that occurred in China.The local media had numerous reports on the atrocious detail of crimes. Repeated reports led the surviving children to recall the exceptionally painful experience. They had to live under an intense psychological pressure. Secondly, too many details in crime news allow people attempting to commit crime to learn the criminal methods and consequently increasing the crime rate. It not only to victim's mental health caused some damage, but also to the whole community to bring about a great potential threat. So the media should not report too many details of crimes to the public. 

On the other hand, the crime news can serve as a public warning. People can enhance their guarding consciousness in order to avoid becoming a victim, and also  assist the police in remaining alert. For example, the media report criminal news in a public place to arouse our social concern. The police can use this information to strengthen the force of supervision in this areas.

In conclusion, I argue that detail of crimes in the media are likely to invite various problems, and we should limit them to protect people from harm.

At present, the question of why fewer farms for their livelihoods in the countryside remains a conundrum. I will give my own points about the causes of this trend and whether it is good for youngsters to work in farming.

prompt: Many people have jobs that involve working shifts (i.e. working at night). What are some of the problems that shift workers might face? What can be done to solve them?
essay:
There is no denying that working shifts might be harder than other regular jobs. This essay is intended to analyze the several drawbacks this category has to face daily, as well as to suggest some solutions to reduce the burden on workers. 

Firstly, there are numerous issues when considering working shifts. In fact, if it is not always pleasant to work, it is much less enjoyable when it is due during nights, bank holidays or weekends. While ordinary employees have the chance to spend festive moments with friends and relatives, healthcare workers, to give an example, are going to the hospital. Year after year, these missed precious moments contribute to a potential burn-out. Moreover, it has been demonstrated that working at night alters the circadian rhythm, which has negative consequences on the physical and psychological sphere. To conclude, these are just a few of the innumerable concerns shift workers have to deal with.

On the other hand, there are different solutions to at least sweeten shift-workers'workers conditions. For instance, an economic incentive should be given to them, especially when considering weekends and bank holidays. In addition, the number of wards should be limited following the guidelines written by the Society of occupational medicine. Finally, extra days off should be guaranteed, in order for workers to spend a proper amount of time with families and friends while they are not sleeping, as well as to profit from daylight to accomplish ordinary tasks. Perhaps, with those suggestions they might cherish their employment a bit more. 

To conclude, there are countless cons to working during shifts. However, there are luckily a bunch of options, alternatives and ideas to ameliorate the condition of this essential class of workers.

prompt: Some people say in order to prevent illness and disease, governments should focus on reducing environmental pollution and housing problem. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
It is argued that governments should prioritize addressing environmental pollution and housing issues as a means to prevent health-related problems. From my perspective, I largely agree with this statement.

On the one hand, there are several methods to deter illness and disease that the government can invest in. Biological research or clinical study could be taken as prominent examples. These studies potentially lead to a better understanding, or better yet, new knowledge about different aspects of health issues, thereby preventing illness and eliminating the roots of it efficiently. However, these research plans may incur substantial costs due to the need for numerous facilities. Consequently, governments might expend on these domains but do not necessarily focus on them.

On the other hand, an investment in reducing environmental problems and house-related ones are seemingly more effective than the aforementioned. First and foremost, people are exposed to the surrounding environment, including both public areas and houses, on a daily basis. hence, if there are some evironment issues such as air pollution or water pollution, their health is immediately impacted profoundly; and because of this, when such problems are addressed, the benefits will quickly come as a result. For instance, when air pollution in Hanoi is reduced due to municipal initiatives, reports indicate a decrease in the number of respiratory-related patients. Ultimately, it is more urgent to tackle issues related to the environment and housing.

tasks). Why is this? Do you think this is a positive or negative development?
essay:
In recent times, many people want their work to be done immediately. I think this is because of the internet, which many people used to do their tasks within minutes. In my opinion, While it has made life easier for many people, others could suffer from chronic illness.

Now, some people are used to the speed; this is due to the existence of the internet in their life. In fact, many governmental institutions migrated to the internet because by doing this, many people can pay online and avoid spending most of their time in long boring queues. As a result, some people tended to do their work with a couple of screen clicks.

On the bright side, doing tasks in no time has made many people feel comfortable. For example, before the invention of the internet, people who were carrying out research had been struggling to find the information. In other words, people instead of spending days at the library trying to get the correct data, it takes a couple of minutes over the internet to find it. Despite finishing work almost immediately has its positives; however, some people suffer from chronic diseases.

By contrast, if many people tried to finish work promptly, they would most likely suffer from stress. As a matter of fact, these people who are trying to deliver their tasks quickly will often negatively affect their health. As a consequence, as long as people are always stressed, they will most probably struggle with chronic diseases such as Hypertension and Diabetes.

To conclude, life has become faster due to the existence of the internet. Personally, I believe that even though this trend has positively affected life as it allows many people to find what they are looking for rapidly, it has some drawbacks because it makes others always stressed, which can result in chronic diseases.

prompt: Some people think that the main purpose of schools is to turn children into good citizens and workers, rather than to benefit them as individuals. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
Some people are of the opinion that the primary role of schools is to create good citizens, instead of merely working towards individual betterment. During the course of this essay, this statement would be subjected to objective discussion before drawing a rational conclusion. 

Obviously, there are a plethora of reasons to support the given perspective; nevertheless, the value of a well-functioning society seems to be the most widely accepted. As contemporary society moves into a phase of rapid developments in medicine, technology, welfare and so on, the luxury of conformity requires people to sacrifice their individualism for common prosperity.  To be specific, individuals are expected to function harmoniously to both stimulate the economy and preserve the peaceful existence amongst communities. As much as these prerequisites require education, school is where this education can initiate. In many educational institutions, teachers have the power to both openly and subconsciously influence students’ socially responsible actions and provide them with a solid foundation for future employment.

On the other hand, this perspective should not be considered an ideal one. As a result of following their own ostensibly selfish goals, students who are taught to share, work effectively in teams, and enhance their talents can benefit society. In addition, it’s possible that occasionally the emphasis on society may come at the expense of people’s mental health. For instance, pupils who are instructed to keep their personal opinions to themselves in class, as is typical in many authoritarian countries, could harbour anger and feel unable to properly express themselves in a democratic setting. According to cognitive science, the ability to express oneself without worrying about negative consequences or repercussions is essential for well-rounded development.

In conclusion, while there is a case to be made for societal stability, schools should work to develop strong, motivated students. Naturally, these objectives do not conflict with one another, and it is important to highlight the overlap.

prompt: Some people think the increasing business and cultural contact between countries brings many positive effects. Others say it causes the loss of national identities. Discuss both view and give your opinion.
essay:
The era of globalisation has made nations around to globe to become interdependent in terms of politics, education, medicine especially international trade and culture exchange. Many regard that the rise in economic and traditional diversity of one nation possess beneficial impacts upon others. However, some object to this idea because of the visible possibility that indigenous values may be perished. 

        To begin with, numerous developing countries have been experiencing a rise in employment opportunity and a massive economic gains with regard to globalisation. The modern business has now found its capability to approach customers of oversea countries. To clearly explain this, companies possess a greater chance to have their products increased in sales numbers and to modify their items to become more approachable and more versatile. Furthermore, the cultural relationships among countries allow employees to go abroad and learn more new things about their neighbours. For example, the Americans usually take trips to Vietnam to experience a wide variety of exclusive foods and learn about the history of their past rival. These people exploit this prospect to improve their foreign foods’ quality, especially Pho and to enhance USA’s historical educational quality.

       However wonderful the advantages may be, foreign policies may terminate a country’s exclusiveness. Globalization has provided a more diverse world in terms of culture’s quantity. One culture may become dominating and make others disappear. Once the culture of a region completely vanishes, it will reverse the merits which globalization evokes. For instance, if Vietnam’s indigenous food like Pho disappears, the amount of international tourists will disappear. Therefore, the tourism industry in Vietnam will fall into chaos and thereby downgrading the whole economic system.

     In conclusion, though globalisation may benefit countries in terms of the economy, those countries, neglecting its policies about such progress might one day disappear completely off the world map. From my own experience, it is up to every individual’s awareness to not only keeps their identity but also encourages national growth.

prompt: Some people think that employers should not care about the way their employees dress, because what matters is the quality of their work. To what extent do you agree or disagree
essay:
In this modern workplace, the trend of dress codes is changing as employers focus more on results than rules that employees must follow. Although I agree that people should dress formally in some professional settings, there is no one-size-fits-all answer for every industry.

On the one hand, professional attire plays a key role in certain professions, such as the hospitality industry, and healthcare sectors, to name but a few, which is considered a must for many jobs. For example, clients are able to recognize waiters or waitresses at a hotel by clothing, thus facilitating work procedures and saving time and effort. The uniform is also a norm among soldiers and police officers, which can not only make them easily identified but signalize the image of authority and trustworthiness. Furthermore, formal wear is usually required on some occasions to show our respect and professionalism to others. For instance, interviewees should put on office attire in the meeting, thereby impressing interviewers with professional attitudes. 

On the other hand, the uniform may deprive us of creativity and the pursuit of freedom, which should not be de rigueur in all contexts. It is par for the course for employees working in high-tech companies to wear casually. After all, they do not need to serve customers face-to-face or present seriousness to others. More importantly, dress codes should not steal the show in the workplace, instead, they must place high emphasis on the work results. Furthermore, with the increasing number of people working at home and becoming self-employed, it is unnecessary to regulate subordinates what to wear at work. 

In conclusion, while business clothes should not reign supreme in all businesses, it is still the order of the day in many professions according to professional settings and requirements.

prompt: Some people think that one of the best ways to solve environmental problems is to increase the cost of fuel. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
The world is now surrounded by many urgent environmental problems, and many people feel that raising the cost of fuel is one of the most effective ways of combatting them. Although I tend to agree with this viewpoint, I believe there are other environmental measures that need to be taken. 

Increasing petrol prices would help save the environment in many ways. As the number of vehicles on the roads is increasing dramatically, so is the amount of vehicular emissions released into the atmosphere. However, if governments increase fuel costs, this solution would serve as a deterrent for people who drive their private cars, reducing air pollution and ozone layer depletion. Besides discouraging people from driving their private cars, this would also generate income to make further investments in environmental initiatives. Given these potential benefits of raising fuel prices, is it hardly surprising to see so many countries applying it to practice.

Apart from increasing the cost of fuel, we can also implement other solutions to environmental issues. For instance, on an individual level, people who want to help the environment should become more environmentally responsible by buying eco-friendly products, and reusing and recycling plastic bags. They can also take public transport as opposed to their personal cars, which produce toxic fumes. From a government’s perspective, it is essential to switch to green energy sources, such as solar and wind, rather than continuing to rely on gas and petrol. Thus, environmental protection goes beyond just raising fuel prices. 

In conclusion, though I attach great importance to the practice of increasing fuel costs, I think that both individuals and governing bodies should become environment-oriented in their mindset to tackle environmental problems.
evaluation:
**Task Achievement:**

The essay effectively addresses the given task by discussing both the extent to which increasing fuel costs can help solve environmental problems and the need for other environmental measures. The ideas presented are clear, relevant, and coherent. The essay adequately covers all aspects of the task and provides appropriate arguments and evidence.

**Suggested Band Score (Task Achievement): 8.0**

essay:
In this modern era , everyone needs money for the their survival and plays an indispensable role . After making money , organization have some social duty . I personally believe that a business should pay attention towards social responsibility.

To begin with , enterprises have many responsibility regarding society and they need to fulfil it because a business operates in a society. Besides this ,  a business  gets resources from society , thus it's duty of a organization to pay back to society.  For instance , if a enterprise gets its raw material 
from community than a company will provide good quality product in return. Secondly, if a business perform it's duty very well then it will somehow benefits the entire community as well as enterprise, because it will increase the sale , additionally reputation of company will become good.

On the other hand , sometime social responsibility  creates loss for a company as  when they organise a  free health check up or give charity etc because all this money is withdrawn from company's profit . However , company by doing it's social responsibility generates employment , so whenever a new enterprise  starts it give job to numerous people.

To conclude, it's a duty of company to fulfil it's social responsibility as somehow it will benefit the organization as well as community. Moreover ,it's better to perform their responsibility because it will create ample opportunities not only for community but for enterprise.
